headline: Patriot costs may double; delivery pushed to 2031–2034.
summary: The US formally notified Switzerland of a further 5–7 year delay (on top of the previously announced 4–5 year slip) and a potential cost jump from CHF 2.3B to ~CHF 4.6B, attributed to Iran-war production demands.

headline: Switzerland–US trade framework: no deal signed; talks continue.
summary: The November 2025 framework capping Swiss tariffs at 15% required a final agreement by 31 March 2026.

headline: Vaud submits CHF 207.6M Grand Council request for shared digital patient record.
summary: The cantonal government is asking for financing for DPI VD — an integrated record system shared by CHUV (CHF 104.5M) and eleven FHV hospitals (CHF 53.1M state guarantee), plus CHF 50M for a transversal project team.

headline: National 142 domestic violence hotline live; Vaud pilots GPS bracelets in H2 2026.
summary: Switzerland's emergency line for domestic violence went live 1 May.

headline: June 14 population-cap referendum: 52% in favour.
summary: Latest polling shows 52% support / 46% opposed for the initiative to cap Switzerland's permanent resident population at 10 million by 2050.

headline: Trump–Xi summit opens in Beijing (Day 1 of 2).
summary: Trump arrived 13 May with Tim Cook, Elon Musk, and Jensen Huang in tow; formal talks began at the Great Hall of the People on 14 May.

headline: Iran–Hormuz stalemate: 5% shipping, US fires on tankers, Pakistan mediates.
summary: The US naval blockade (in force since 13 April) has reduced Strait traffic to ~5% of pre-conflict levels; 52 vessels turned back.

headline: India–Pakistan: ceasefire holds at one year; Indus Waters Treaty still suspended.
summary: Pakistan marked the anniversary of the May 2025 conflict; India has not reinstated the Indus Waters Treaty, which underwrites 240M people's irrigation.

headline: EU defence ministers meet this week on Ukraine and Middle East.
summary: Week of 11–16 May: EU defence ministers discuss military support for Ukraine and readiness in light of the Hormuz crisis; education ministers finalize a partial mandate on Erasmus+ 2028–2034; African–European parliamentarians meeting in Eswatini on security and critical raw materials.

headline: Oxford demonstrates first "quadsqueezing" quantum interaction.
summary: Using a single trapped ion, Oxford physicists produced squeezing, trisqueezing, and — for the first time on any platform — quadsqueezing (a fourth-order quantum effect), achieved 100× faster than conventional approaches.

headline: JWST "little red dots" explained as nano-black-holes in gas cocoons — competing theory live.
summary: [disputed] University of Copenhagen researchers published in *Nature* that hundreds of early-universe red anomalies are young black holes ~100× less massive than expected, swaddled in ionised gas.

headline: FDA approves vepdegestrant (Veppanu) for ESR1-mutated breast cancer.
summary: Approved 1 May 2026 for ER+/HER2−/ESR1-mutated advanced or metastatic breast cancer — a subset that previously had few oral targeted options.

headline: NASA Psyche Mars gravity assist tomorrow (15 May).
summary: The Psyche spacecraft will pass within 4,500 km of Mars, using the flyby to redirect toward target metallic asteroid 16 Psyche.

headline: arXiv:2505.13510
summary: (May 2026) — *"On the definition and importance of interpretability in scientific machine learning"* — Argues current ML interpretability definitions conflate distinct desiderata; proposes clearer taxonomy for scientific (not commercial) settings.

headline: "Token Arena: A Continuous Benchmark Unifying Energy and Cognition in AI Inference"
summary: (cs.AI, May 2026 batch) — Proposes a joint benchmark scoring models on both energy consumption and reasoning performance per token; useful for efficient-inference comparisons across architectures.

headline: "Physically Native World Models: A Hamiltonian Perspective on Generative World Modeling"
summary: (cs.AI, May 2026 batch) — Embeds Hamiltonian energy-conservation priors into a generative world model, producing a hybrid physics/neural architecture that respects physical symmetries.

headline: "SoftSAE: Dynamic Top-K Selection for Adaptive Sparse Autoencoders"
summary: (cs.LG, May 2026 batch) — Replaces the fixed-k sparsity constraint in sparse autoencoders (used in mechanistic interpretability) with a dynamic threshold, improving feature monosemanticity.

headline: Asian equities closed mixed-to-positive.
summary: Asia-Pacific up ~0.2%; South Korea (KOSPI) led +1.7%.

headline: EUR/CHF ~0.9146–0.9176; USD/CHF ~0.777–0.783 this week.
summary: ECB FX feed returned HTTP 403; rates via MTFX historical.

headline: US futures pointing higher pre-open.
